LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT SUSAN LEFF'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN BURDEN THAT STATEMENT IS PROTECTED WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 425.16 AND THE POLICE OFFICER'S CONDUCT IS AN ISSUE OF PUBLIC SIGNIFICANCE SUBJECT TO NY TIMES VS. SULLIVAN TEST. LAW AND MOTION 301, PREVIOUSLY SUBMITTED ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2007, DEFENDANT SUSAN LEFF'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. ATTY'S FEES IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $24,000. - JUDGE PETER J. BUSCH

ATTY. ALEXANDRA V. ATENCIO OF HANSON BRIDGETT, TEL. NO. 995-5870 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY SUSAN LEE.

RULING - A R G U E D; JUDGE BUSCH DISCLOSED THAT HIS WIFE, CATHARINE BARNES, IS A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY AND NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE. NO OBJECTIONS WERE MADE. DEFENDANT SUSAN LEFF'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN BURDEN THAT STATEMENT IS PROTECTED WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 425.16 AND THE POLICE OFFICER'S CONDUCT IS AN ISSUE OF PUBLIC SIGNIFICANCE SUBJECT TO NY TIMES VS. SULLIVAN TEST.
